note: Layer-2 acceptance for the spec self-consistency gate — the T3 slice shipped as PR #189. The behavioral surface is the CI check itself, so each item is a deterministic script run. Rev 4 (2026-07-21) replaced the PR-state coupling with artifact self-consistency (owner decision D1, Option A), which retired items T1-T4 and T6: they asserted a rule the gate no longer carries. The new items exercise the paths that actually block. Every item below is reproducible offline from a clean checkout — no gh, no network, no live PR.

items.title: Fixture suite passes — 22 named cases covering every ledger defect and both must-not-fire classes
items.precondition: Clean checkout of claude/ci-spec-parity-gate; python3 available. No repo state or network needed.
items.expected: exit 0; every case prints PASS; the summary reads '22 passed, 0 failed, 22 cases'
items.evidence: 22 passed, 0 failed, 22 cases; exit 0. Cases include pill-completed-card-pending, card-only-completed, trailing-text-completed-17-of-17, single-quoted-class, class-reordered, pill-wrapped-across-lines, diff-header-collision, nested-dot-span-template-b, template-b-meta-pills-not-mistaken-for-status, and the two false-positive classes reindent-only-completed-pill and class-swap-pending-to-completed-text-unchanged (both exit 0).
items.notes: Fixtures are generated into a temp directory at run time rather than committed, so the suite is self-contained and cannot go stale against a missing file.

items.title: Gate BLOCKS — §20.5 breach: a change moves a spec to Completed with a pending acceptance item
items.precondition: Two real commits: base = the remediated branch, head = a commit flipping this plan's spec.html to Completed while test-results.json still carries a pending item.
steps: 1. git checkout -b tmp/proof && flip the hero pill and status card to Completed, set one acceptance item to 'pending', commit
items.expected: exit 1, naming the spec path, the offending acceptance item, and §20.5
items.evidence: Observed exit 1. Output: "FINDING plans/07192026-handoff-enforcement-system/spec.html — status is 'Completed' but the acceptance artifact does not roll up Passed: item T5 is 'pending' with no waiver (§20.5)." followed by "check-spec-parity: FAIL — spec status is not self-consistent." Fixture branch deleted after the run; no branch pushed.
items.notes: This is the item finding 10 said did not exist. The previous artifact had no item in which the gate exited non-zero on the path the workflow runs.

items.title: Gate BLOCKS — pill and status card disagree
items.precondition: Head commit flips the hero pill to 'Blocked' and leaves the status card reading 'In Progress'.
steps: 1. Commit the one-surface edit on a throwaway branch
items.expected: exit 1, naming the file and BOTH extracted values (§3.3)
items.evidence: Observed exit 1. Output: "FINDING plans/07192026-handoff-enforcement-system/spec.html — hero pill says 'blocked' but the status card says 'in progress' — AGENTS.md requires both to be updated together." This is finding 5: the previous script read only the pill and passed this diff clean.

items.title: Gate FAILS CLOSED — a touched spec with no extractable status exits 3, not 0
items.precondition: Head commit removes the status pill and the status card from a touched spec.html.
steps: 1. Strip both status surfaces on a throwaway branch and commit
items.expected: exit 3; names the file and the surfaces searched; never prints 'nothing to assert'
items.evidence: Observed exit 3. Output: "FAIL-CLOSED plans/07192026-handoff-enforcement-system/spec.html — no status found. Searched: <span class=\"status-pill\">, <span class=\"pill\">, and the status-card <dd>." This is finding 8: the previous script returned exit 0 with a reassuring message on any parse failure.

items.title: Gate does NOT fire on a brush — spec edited, status untouched
items.precondition: Head commit edits a spec.html body without changing either status surface.
steps: 1. Append an unrelated comment to the spec on a throwaway branch and commit
items.evidence: Observed exit 0: "check-spec-parity: 1 spec.html checked, all self-consistent." Covers the false-positive class of finding 7 on a real diff rather than a fixture.

items.title: Infrastructure failure exits 2 and never reports clean
steps: 3. bash scripts/check-spec-parity.sh --base deadbeef --head HEAD   # unresolvable base
items.expected: exit 2 in all four cases; no 'nothing to assert' line on any of them
items.evidence: All four returned exit 2. The python3-absent case printed 'check-spec-parity: python3 not found — cannot parse specs, refusing to report clean'; grep for 'nothing to assert' across the output returned 0 matches.

items.title: Corpus sweep — every spec.html in the repo is self-consistent
items.expected: exit 0; zero findings; off-vocabulary statuses reported as advisory warnings only
items.evidence: 123 spec.html checked, exit 0, zero pill/card disagreements, zero fail-closed files. Nine advisory warnings across five specs: 'implemented' x2 specs, 'superseded', 'deployed to dev', 'completed locally'. The spec's predicted '4 files where pill and card disagree' is not reproducible — with head-token normalization the corpus has none.
items.notes: The spec's corpus figures were measured as 111 files with 1 lacking a status pill. The real base carries 123 files, of which 10 use a second hero template. Supporting that template is what keeps the fail-closed rule from turning 10 specs red.

items.title: The gate passes on its own originating spec
items.precondition: This plan folder as shipped by PR #189.
items.evidence: exit 0 — '1 spec.html checked, all self-consistent.' Pill and status card both read 'In Progress'.
items.notes: The minimum bar named in the remediation spec's T8.

items.title: The armed PR-LOG step is gone from this workflow
items.evidence: grep -c returned 0. The step that would have become a hard blocking gate on every PR the moment scripts/pr-log.sh landed from PR #181 no longer exists in this workflow.
items.notes: PR-LOG freshness belongs to the PR that owns the script (#181). Recorded here because it was the highest-severity item in the remediation ledger.

items.title: Workflow runs on real CI after the rewrite
items.precondition: Remediated workflow pushed to claude/ci-spec-parity-gate; pull_request -> dev/dave.
steps: 2. Read the 'PR Spec Checks' run on PR #189
items.expected: run conclusion success; the checkout fetches base and head; the parity step logs a real verdict rather than a nothing-to-assert line
items.evidence: GitHub Actions run 29769554597 on head 735169c27, event pull_request, conclusion success. The 'Fetch the two commits the check compares' step resolved BASE_SHA 4cdb0eafa and HEAD_SHA 735169c27 at --depth=1. The parity step logged 'check-spec-parity: 1 spec.html checked, all self-consistent.' — a real verdict on a real touched spec, not the previous 'sets no spec.html to a done status — nothing to assert' no-op line.
items.notes: This is the distinction finding 10 turned on: the earlier run banked green from a branch the gate never exercised. Here the parity step parsed a touched spec and reported on it.
